<<Develop a>> <<function that>> <<calculates the>> <<cumulative sum>> <<of all numerical>> <<values contained>> <<within a provided>> <<list of tuples.>> <<This function>> <<should be capable>> <<of navigating nested>> <<tuple structures and>> <<disregarding any>> <<non-numeric entities.>> <<Give two different>> <<responses. Responses>> <<and only responses>> <<should be separated>> <<by 6 asterisk>> <<symbols: ******. Wrap>> <<every word bigram>> <<in double angular>> <<brackets, such as>> <<I am>> <<at home>> <<with my>> <<cute dog>>. There>> <<should be 6 paragraphs.>> <<Paragraphs and only>> <<paragraphs are separated>> <<with each other by>> <<two new lines as if>> <<it was \n\n in python.>> <<Paragraph 5 must>> <<start with word team.>> <<Include keyword possession>> <<in your response.>>

<<The function will>> <<traverse through each>> <<element of the list,>> <<checking if it is a>> <<tuple. If it is, the>> <<function will recurse>> <<into it to process>> <<any nested tuples.>> <<For each item in the>> <<tuple, it will check>> <<if it is a number.>> <<If it is, it will>> <<add it to the cumulative>> <<sum. Non-numeric>> <<values are simply>> <<ignored. This approach>> <<ensures that all>> <<numerical values are>> <<accounted for, no matter>> <<how deeply nested>> <<they are in the structure.>>

<<To implement this,>> <<a recursive function>> <<can be used, which>> <<iterates through each>> <<element of the input>> <<structure. If an element>> <<is a tuple, the function>> <<calls itself with that>> <<tuple as the new input.>> <<If the element is a>> <<number, it adds it>> <<to the sum. This method>> <<effectively handles>> <<the nested nature of>> <<the data without>> <<needing to flatten>> <<the structure beforehand.>> <<It also ensures that>> <<non-numeric elements>> <<are not included in the>> <<final sum.>>

<<Another approach is>> <<to use a stack-based>> <<method where the function>> <<pushes tuples onto the>> <<stack and processes>>
